quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Mole Concepts: Molecules Removed and Percent Solutions
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Molecules to Mass, Mass to Molecules, Removal of Molecules Calculations, Percent by Weight Solutions, Hydrated Compounds

questions.question: If $6 \times 10^{21}$ molecules are removed from 0.5 g of $\text{O}_3$, how many moles remain?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass O₃ = 48.00 g/mol
Initial moles = 0.5 / 48.00 = 0.01042 mol
Initial molecules = 0.01042 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $6.27 \times 10^{21}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $6.27 \times 10^{21} - 6 \times 10^{21} = 2.7 \times 10^{20}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $2.7 \times 10^{20} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.00448$ mol

questions.question: The mass of $2.5 \times 10^{22}$ molecules of $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $2.5 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0415$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass H₂SO₄ = 98.08 g/mol
Mass = 0.0415 × 98.08 = 4.07 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{KNO}_3$ must be dissolved in 90 g of water to prepare a 30% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of KNO₃
Solution mass = 90 + x
30% = x / (90 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.30(90 + x) = x
27 + 0.30x = x
27 = 0.70x
x = 27 / 0.70 = 38.57 g ≈ 38.6 g

questions.question: If $1 \times 10^{22}$ molecules are removed from 4 g of $\text{N}_2$, what is the number of moles left?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass N₂ = 28.02 g/mol
Initial moles = 4 / 28.02 = 0.1428 mol
Initial molecules = 0.1428 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $8.60 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $8.60 \times 10^{22} - 1 \times 10^{22} = 7.60 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $7.60 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.126$ mol

questions.question: The weight of $6 \times 10^{21}$ molecules of $\text{NaHCO}_3$ is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $6 \times 10^{21} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.00996$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass NaHCO₃ = 84.01 g/mol
Mass = 0.00996 × 84.01 = 0.837 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{CuSO}_4 \cdot 5\text{H}_2\text{O}$ must be dissolved in 75 g of water to make a 15% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of CuSO₄·5H₂O
Solution mass = 75 + x
15% = x / (75 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.15(75 + x) = x
11.25 + 0.15x = x
11.25 = 0.85x
x = 11.25 / 0.85 = 13.24 g ≈ 13.2 g

questions.question: If $3 \times 10^{22}$ molecules are removed from 2.5 g of $\text{CH}_4$, how many moles remain?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass CH₄ = 16.04 g/mol
Initial moles = 2.5 / 16.04 = 0.1559 mol
Initial molecules = 0.1559 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $9.39 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $9.39 \times 10^{22} - 3 \times 10^{22} = 6.39 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $6.39 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.106$ mol

questions.question: The mass of $4 \times 10^{23}$ molecules of sucrose ($\text{C}_{12}\text{H}_{22}\text{O}_{11}$) is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $4 \times 10^{23} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.664$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass sucrose = 342.30 g/mol
Mass = 0.664 × 342.30 = 227 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{LiCl}$ must be dissolved in 60 g of water to make a 20% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of LiCl
Solution mass = 60 + x
20% = x / (60 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.20(60 + x) = x
12 + 0.20x = x
12 = 0.80x
x = 12 / 0.80 = 15.0 g

questions.question: If $5 \times 10^{21}$ molecules are removed from 0.8 g of $\text{CO}_2$, how many moles remain?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol
Initial moles = 0.8 / 44.01 = 0.01818 mol
Initial molecules = 0.01818 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $1.095 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $1.095 \times 10^{22} - 0.5 \times 10^{22} = 5.95 \times 10^{21}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $5.95 \times 10^{21} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.00988$ mol

questions.question: The weight of $9 \times 10^{22}$ molecules of $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2$ is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $9 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.1495$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass H₂O₂ = 34.02 g/mol
Mass = 0.1495 × 34.02 = 5.09 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{NaNO}_3$ must be dissolved in 150 g of water to prepare a 25% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of NaNO₃
Solution mass = 150 + x
25% = x / (150 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.25(150 + x) = x
37.5 + 0.25x = x
37.5 = 0.75x
x = 37.5 / 0.75 = 50.0 g

questions.question: If $7 \times 10^{21}$ molecules are removed from 1.6 g of $\text{CH}_3\text{OH}$, how many moles remain?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass CH₃OH = 32.04 g/mol
Initial moles = 1.6 / 32.04 = 0.04994 mol
Initial molecules = 0.04994 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $3.007 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $3.007 \times 10^{22} - 0.7 \times 10^{22} = 2.307 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $2.307 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0383$ mol

questions.question: The mass of $1.2 \times 10^{22}$ molecules of acetaldehyde ($\text{C}_2\text{H}_4\text{O}$) is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $1.2 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.01993$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass C₂H₄O = 44.05 g/mol
Mass = 0.01993 × 44.05 = 0.878 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{BaCl}_2 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$ must be dissolved in 200 g of water to prepare a 10% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of BaCl₂·2H₂O
Solution mass = 200 + x
10% = x / (200 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.10(200 + x) = x
20 + 0.10x = x
20 = 0.90x
x = 20 / 0.90 = 22.22 g ≈ 22.2 g

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered complex mole concept applications! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review molecule removal calculations and percent solution setup.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting between molecules and moles correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review Avogadro's number, molar mass calculations, and percent by weight formulas.
